## Yahoo Finance / CryptoProwl

Robinhood’s Prediction Market Will Overtake Crypto, Says Bernstein
CryptoProwl
Tue, July 21, 2026 at 1:58 AM GMT+8

Analysts at Wall Street brokerage Bernstein say it won't be long before Robinhood Markets is earning more revenue from its prediction market than crypto trading.

In a note to clients, Bernstein raised its price target on HOOD stock to $160 U.S. from $130 U.S. and reiterated an outperform rating.

Analyst Gautam Chhugani said Robinhood is likely to benefit from $70 billion U.S. of trading on prediction markets, perpetual futures contracts, and tokenized equities.

Chhugani expects Robinhood to grow annual prediction market revenue at a 64% compound annual rate through 2028, reaching $1.7 billion U.S. Robinhood's prediction market got a big boost from the FIFA World Cup, which accounted for 93% of trading volumes in June and early July. Robinhood's prediction market became the fourth largest by trading volume within a month of launch, according to the report.

---

## CNBC video

Bernstein’s Ian Moore on why prediction markets are outpacing sports books

Ian Moore, Bernstein, joins Closing Bell Overtime to discuss prediction markets outpacing sportsbooks during the World Cup and what is ahead for prediction markets and sports betting.

---

## iGamingToday

Sportsbooks Lose Ground as Prediction Markets Secure 27% of World Cup Action
Chidubem Ovute
July 20, 2026

Prediction markets grew sharply during the World Cup. Industry data from H2 Gambling Capital estimated prediction-market trading at about 27% of legal U.S. sports-betting volume in the first month of the tournament, up from 9% at the start of 2026. The article noted the comparison is imperfect because prediction markets and sportsbooks track activity differently.

Kalshi’s app reportedly drew more daily users than DraftKings and FanDuel, according to Apptopia. Kalshi said it attracted 3 million new users and drove $1.2 billion in trading on its World Cup champion market.

A review by Citizens JMP Securities measured implied vig across six operators. It reported:

- Polymarket: 2.70%
- Kalshi: 4.71%
- DraftKings: 4.97%
- FanDuel: 5.07%
- BetMGM: 5.64%
- Fanatics: 6.14%

Analysts Jordan Bender and Isabella Slavin attributed the shift to heavier trading volume, deeper market-maker participation and competition among liquidity providers. The article states this was the second major event where Kalshi outpriced DraftKings and FanDuel, following March Madness.

Source shown by the article: NEXT.io.

---

## Financial IT

iPredicta Launches Comparison Platform for U.S. Prediction Markets
20 July 2026

iPredicta launched a dedicated comparison platform for U.S. prediction markets. The affiliate-model site compares live prices across multiple venues, including Polymarket, and sends users onward to participating exchanges to register and trade.

At launch it tracked dozens of live markets across two venues, with automated updates. It scores markets based on liquidity and uncertainty and sends price-change notifications.

The platform includes an AI explanation layer that translates market questions, resolution criteria and live data into plain English. The company says the AI produces words, not numbers, and does not set scores, predict outcomes or give trading advice.

Founder Charlotte Nimmo previously ran Best of Bets and founded Cavalier Public Relations.

## Voces AmeliCA / syndicated YouTube page

Polymarket Is A Bigger Scam Than We Thought
21 July 2026

The page republishes a video description alleging that Polymarket and Kalshi are exploited by insiders and that a Wall Street Journal investigation found influencers were paid to use a fake version of the platform to appear to win large sums. The description is highly opinionated and includes political allegations. Treat as sentiment and distribution evidence, not an authoritative source.

---

## Marin Independent Journal commentary

Californian Voice: Prediction markets need guardrails now, not later
William Lu
Published July 20, 2026

Only the introduction was visible without registration. The article frames prediction markets as another technology category growing faster than public understanding. Treat as public-perception evidence rather than a factual source unless the full article is obtained.

---

## Organ Mountain News

Heinrich urges tighter limits on prediction markets to protect tribal gaming
20 July 2026

U.S. Sen. Martin Heinrich and 11 Senate colleagues urged leaders of the Senate Banking and Agriculture committees to amend digital-asset legislation to protect tribal authority and state gambling regulation.

The senators asked Congress to preserve Indian Gaming Regulatory Act protections and prohibit CFTC-regulated entities from listing prediction contracts resembling sports bets or casino games.

The letter was supported by the Indian Gaming Association, National Congress of American Indians and 17 New Mexico tribal governments. Senators joining Heinrich included Tina Smith, Maria Cantwell, Patty Murray, Richard Blumenthal, Mark Kelly, Tammy Baldwin, Alex Padilla, Adam Schiff, Jacky Rosen, Brian Schatz and Gary Peters.

The report says the proposed safeguards would clarify that federal digital-asset legislation does not override tribal authority or tribal-state compacts and would bar online sports wagering and event-based prediction markets where tribes hold gaming rights.
note: from Stuart's 2026-07-21 V10/FLAT intel pack (lossless capture)
